key_insight: A boundary-masked, physics-informed attention mechanism improves out-of-distribution grain-growth prediction by focusing the model on grain boundaries, while still generalizing without retraining.
engineering_relevance: This suggests deep learning models trained on synthetic microstructure data can be deployed more reliably for real materials states, with better accuracy when the boundary morphology remains similar to the training domain.
method: Compare a baseline deep-learning grain-growth model and a physics-informed attention variant
Mask attention to grain-boundary pixels to encode curvature-driven grain-growth behavior
Evaluate both models on three OOD cases without retraining or fine-tuning
evidence_signal: On bimodal grain-size microstructures, SSIM improved from 0.6221 to 0.7609 and mean grain-size error dropped from 8.75% to 3.57%, with successful generalization across experimental, bimodal, and abnormal grain-growth cases.
watch_item: The gain depends on how well the test microstructure boundary morphology matches the synthetic training distribution, so extreme morphology shifts may reduce the benefit.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Attention Mechanism and Generalization Capability of Deep Learning-Based Grain Growth Evolution Prediction
